qa_pairs.question: What is TensorBoard?
qa_pairs.answer: TensorBoard is a suite of web applications designed for inspecting and understanding your AI and Machine Learning runs.

qa_pairs.question: What visual tools are included in TensorBoard?
qa_pairs.answer: TensorBoard includes visual tools to track performance and evaluation metrics, profile code, explore intermediate layers inside models, and visualize embeddings, among other features.

qa_pairs.question: Which AI frameworks does TensorBoard support?
qa_pairs.answer: TensorBoard supports frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Jax.

qa_pairs.question: For which framework was TensorBoard originally developed?
qa_pairs.answer: TensorBoard was originally built for TensorFlow.

qa_pairs.question: How can I launch TensorBoard on JupyterHub?
qa_pairs.answer: On clusters where JupyterHub is available, you can launch TensorBoard by clicking on its icon on an active launcher tab.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after launching TensorBoard on JupyterHub?
qa_pairs.answer: After launching TensorBoard on JupyterHub, the application will open on a new tab in your web browser, and a directory named `$HOME/tensorboard_logs` will be created.

qa_pairs.question: What is the default directory where TensorBoard looks for data on JupyterHub?
qa_pairs.answer: Upon launching TensorBoard on JupyterHub, a directory named `$HOME/tensorboard_logs` will be created, and this is where TensorBoard will look for data to display.

qa_pairs.question: What must I do to ensure data is displayed in TensorBoard on JupyterHub?
qa_pairs.answer: You must ensure that any calls to TensorBoard in your code write data to the `$HOME/tensorboard_logs` directory.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I fail to write data to the correct directory for TensorBoard on JupyterHub?
qa_pairs.answer: Failing to write data to the correct directory will result in no data being displayed on the TensorBoard tab in your browser.

qa_pairs.question: How can I change the default log directory for TensorBoard on JupyterHub?
qa_pairs.answer: You can change the location of the TensorBoard log directory by adding `export TENSORBOARD_LOGDIR=/some/other/path` to your `.bashrc` file.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find detailed examples for using TensorBoard with different AI frameworks?
qa_pairs.answer: For detailed examples on the many uses of TensorBoard, you should consult the official documentation for your preferred AI framework.

qa_pairs.question: What is the official documentation link for PyTorch with TensorBoard?
qa_pairs.answer: The official documentation link for PyTorch with TensorBoard is `https://docs.pytorch.org/tutorials/intermediate/tensorboard_tutorial.html`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the official documentation link for TensorFlow with TensorBoard?
qa_pairs.answer: The official documentation link for TensorFlow with TensorBoard is `https://www.tensorflow.org/tensorboard/get_started`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the official documentation link for Jax with TensorBoard?
qa_pairs.answer: The official documentation link for Jax with TensorBoard is `https://docs.jaxstack.ai/en/latest/JAX_visualizing_models_metrics.html`.
